common.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

active.label: Active & healthy
active.desc: Checking in regularly. Nothing for you to do.

grace.label: Check-in overdue
grace.desc: A check-in was missed — a grace period is counting down.

pending.label: Awaiting verification
pending.desc: Grace has lapsed. A death certificate is being reviewed.

released.label: Released to you
released.desc: The instructions left for you are ready to view.

rejected.label: Report needs another look
rejected.desc: The report couldn't be approved — see the note and submit again.

profile.title: Profile
profile.sub: Who you are and how you sign in

home.intro: You've been entrusted with the legacies below. There's nothing you need to do until the time comes — we'll guide you when it does.
home.disabledBody: Everything entrusted to you is safe and untouched. Contact support to restore access.
home.releasedBannerOne: {{count}} legacy has been released to you and is ready to view.
home.releasedBannerMany: {{count}} legacy have been released to you and are ready to view.
home.emptyTitle: No one has named you yet
home.emptyBody: When someone entrusts you with their legacy, they'll appear here.
home.privateFooter: Private to you. Wassiya can never see what's inside — only your key can open it.

key.openInvite: Set it up in a minute — it's how everything stays private to you.

account.title: My key
account.sub: One key unlocks every legacy you've been named in
account.privateBody: Your legacy is locked on your device — openable only with your key or recovery code. We can never see it.

personal.title: Personal
personal.desc: Your name, shown to the people who named you.

security.title: Sign-in password
security.desc: Change the password you use to sign in. Signed in with Google or Apple and never set one? Use "Forgot password" on the sign-in page first.

keyCards.verificationHint: Read it aloud — or let them scan it — so anyone who names you can confirm it's really you.
keyCards.savedWhenSetup: Saved when you set up your key.
keyCards.recoveryHint: It's the only key to everything left for you, and it's never kept here — we can't show it again. If you lose it, it can't be recovered, and a legacy already released to you could no longer be opened. Please keep it somewhere safe now.

rotate.blockedReleased: A legacy has already been released to you. For your protection, the code can't be changed any more — those items can only be opened with the recovery code you saved when you set up.
rotate.blockedOwnerUnreachable: We can't reset your code right now because someone who named you hasn't checked in recently. Please try again later, or ask them to open their app.
rotate.fingerprintBody: Your verification code has changed. When you next speak to anyone who named you, read them this new code so they know it's still you.
rotate.dialogDesc: We can give you a fresh one. Your old code and old key will stop working. The people who named you don't need to do anything — everything left for you is re-secured to your new key automatically next time they open their app.
rotate.understand: I understand my old recovery code will no longer work.

benefactor.sealedNote: Contents stay sealed until release — even we can't see them.

footer.released: Ready to view — open to see what was left for you
footer.rejected: The report couldn't be approved — open to see why and submit again
footer.grace: You can report a death to begin verification

cards.releasedBody: Opened privately on your device — only your key can.
cards.rejectedBodyWithDate: A reviewer looked at the report from {{when}} and couldn't approve it. You can submit a corrected one.
cards.rejectedBody: A reviewer looked at the report and couldn't approve it. You can submit a corrected one.
cards.underReviewBodyWithDate: The death report from {{when}} is being checked by a reviewer. You'll be emailed the outcome — nothing more is needed from you right now.
cards.underReviewBody: The death report is being checked by a reviewer. You'll be emailed the outcome — nothing more is needed from you right now.
cards.reportBody: If {{firstName}} has passed, you can report it and submit a death certificate — even if check-ins look healthy. Nothing is released on your word alone — a reviewer must approve it.

report.title: Report the passing of {{firstName}}
report.intro: This begins the verification process. You'll upload a death certificate, which a Wassiya reviewer checks before anything is released. Nothing is released on your word alone.
report.privateNote: The certificate is reviewed privately — it is never added to the vault.
report.fileHint: PDF or photo · up to 10 MB · sent securely
report.fileTooLarge: That file is larger than 10 MB — please use a smaller photo or PDF.
report.submittedBody: A reviewer will verify the certificate. You and the other beneficiaries will be notified of the outcome. Thank you — this is a hard thing to do.

release.heroSub: Opened privately on your device — for you alone. May Allah have mercy on them.
release.notAvailable: This legacy isn't available to view yet.
release.enterCodeBody: This opens what was left for you, privately on this device. We never see your code.
release.wrongCode: That recovery code didn't work. Check it and try again.
release.openFailed: Something went wrong while opening it. Please try again.
release.noItems: No items were sealed for you.
release.faraidNote: Distributed by Fara'id with the rest of the estate. Keep these details private.

invite.invalidBody: Check that the whole link was copied, or ask the person who sent it for a new one.
invite.expiredBodyNamed: Ask {{ownerName}} for a new link — it only takes them a moment.
invite.expiredBody: Ask the person who sent it for a new link — it only takes them a moment.
invite.arriveNote: Invitations arrive as a private link from the person who named you.
invite.panelBody: You've been chosen to receive a loved one's legacy. Sign in to get set up — so their wishes reach you, and no one else.
invite.privateFooter: Private — only you can open it
invite.acceptBody: Sign in or create an account to continue. Signing in gets you into your account; a separate step keeps your legacy private to you.
invite.terms: By continuing you agree to Wassiya's Terms & Privacy. We can never see what's inside.
invite.point1Body: It's yours alone and never leaves this device unprotected.
invite.point2Title: Backed up by a recovery code
invite.point2Body: A recovery code lets you restore it on any device, for years.
invite.point3Body: You'll read a short verification code to them so they know it's you.
invite.errUsed: This invitation has already been used.
invite.errExpired: This invitation has expired. Ask for a new one.
invite.setupBody: Someone has entrusted you with their legacy. Your key is a private code, kept only on this device, that lets you open what's left for you — and no one else can, not even us.
invite.fingerprintBody: Read this verification code to the person who entrusted you — in person or on a call — so they know it's really you, and that no one stepped in.
invite.allSetBody: Your key is ready. What's left for you will be released privately — to you and no one else — if and when the time comes.
invite.generatingSub: Created privately on your device · never sent to us
invite.recoveryBody2: key to everything left for you. Save it somewhere you'll still have it in years — a password manager, written down and kept safe.
invite.qrHint: Prefer a photo? Scan or photograph this code — it holds the same recovery code, and it's made right here on your device.
invite.loseWarning: If you lose it, no one can recover it — not Wassiya, and not even after the person who named you has passed. Their legacy to you would be lost.
invite.savedConfirm: I've saved it, and I understand it's the only copy and can never be recovered.

auth.shellBody: Wassiya keeps your wishes private while you're here, and delivers them to the right people when you're not.
auth.shellFooter: Private — only you can open your vault
auth.signInSub: Sign in to your Wassiya account.
auth.codeSentHint: Sent to {{email}}. It can take a minute to arrive.
auth.createSub: A few seconds now — a lifetime of peace of mind.
auth.passwordHint: At least 10 characters keeps it strong.
auth.verifySub2: to confirm it's really you. Enter it below.
auth.codeArriveHint: It can take a minute to arrive — check spam too.
auth.resetSent2: , a reset link is on its way. Open it on this device to choose a new password.
auth.resetBody: Enter your email and we'll send you a link to choose a new one.
auth.pickNewPassword: Pick a new password for your account.

errors.invalidCredentials: That email or password doesn't match.
errors.userNotFound: We couldn't find an account with that email.
errors.invalidCode: That code doesn't match — check it and try again.
errors.codeExpired: That code has expired — send a new one.
errors.passwordStrength: Please pick a stronger password — longer is better.
errors.userCreation: We couldn't create the account — that email may already be in use.
errors.resetExpired: That reset link has expired — request a new one.
errors.authFailed: Something went wrong. Please try again.

privacy.title: How your privacy works
privacy.sub: Your legacy is private to you and the people you choose — and to no one else.
privacy.point1Body: Everything is locked privately on your device before it's ever saved.
privacy.point2Body: We only ever keep the locked version. No one at Wassiya can see what's inside.
privacy.point3Body: Only your key — or your recovery code — can open what's left for you.
privacy.technical: For the technically curious: everything is sealed with strong end-to-end encryption (AES-256 and RSA). Your private key never leaves your device unprotected, and the servers only ever hold encrypted data.
